response.justification: This candidate has extensive senior software engineering experience, but lacks the specific technical requirements for the role. Their C++ experience ended over 20 years ago (National Instruments, 2000-2001), while the position mandates 5-10 years of current professional C++ development. Their career has pivoted toward TypeScript/Node.js and D-lang, with no mention of Windows platform development or capital markets domain expertise. For a consulting engagement requiring immediate productivity, the ramp-up time to regain C++ proficiency, learn Windows development, and acquire capital markets knowledge would be substantial.
strengths: Senior-level software development background with demonstrated mastery of clean code, TDD, and software craftsmanship principles
Proven ability to learn multiple programming languages and adapt across diverse domains (web, mobile, embedded, GIS)
Strong communication and mentoring skills, with multiple technical workshops and talks delivered
concerns: C++ expertise from 20+ years ago; current primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and D-lang, not C++
No Windows platform development experience; career heavily focused on Linux and web technologies
No capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking domain experience; only one vague reference to general financial services (RIDE Capital)

key_factors.factor: C++ expertise 20+ years stale; mandatory 5-10 years required
Zero capital markets/fixed income domain knowledge (mandatory requirement)
